plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-276 RP08.P02.M03.S01-RP08.P02.M04.S18 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-276 implements descriptor-only coverage of the full RP08.P02.M03 primary implementation service cycle (22 rows including happy/denied/review/integration test rows) and the M04 secondary workflow head (18 rows through retry behavior) across two sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-277 handoff, without opening email/Office-native/sync/filing runtime, review/approval route runtime, rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, idempotency persistence, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-276 (RP08 email-dms-core service slice, risk B, 40 units, RP08.P02.M03.S01-RP08.P02.M04.S18) passes independent read-only review. Contract current_pack, manifest, plan snapshot, and closeout-pack-plan.json all agree on pack identity, unit count, range, deliverable distribution (contract2/impl18/security4/ui6/claude2/recovery4/test4=40), and micro-title split (22/18); validators.js coverage logic enforces these. The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly FALSE (status implemented_pending_review / pending final closeout); the Hermes production_ready_candidate:true is a distinct coverage+slice-valid candidate signal, not a final readiness assertion, so there is no overclaim. No-write/no-real-data boundary holds: zero side-effecting operations in packages/email-dms/src (no writeFile/fetch/exec/process.env/http/child_process), every result Object.frozen with no_real_data:true and all runtime-dispatch/persistence/leak flags false, and validateEmailDmsCoreCp276ServiceSliceDescriptor enforces descriptor-only, customer-safe, no-raw-payload, deny-over-allow, and no-leak guards across both sections. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver (read_only:true, claude_is_final_approval:false, tools Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_review_evidence enumerated, attestation promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic control-plane evidence (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). Review-evidence integrity is sound: readiness ready, auth preflight passed with 1444-byte valid-JSON stdout, no forbidden tools, summary-only correctly prohibited, source inspected via read tools. Unrelated dirty files (LDIP invalid-attempt JSON, .docx, handoff doc) are correctly excluded from pack scope. One non-blocking P3 maintainability observation only. Human final approval for the enterprise trust claim remains required and is outside this read-only review.

findings.title: Service-slice case set reuses CP275 row-extras constant instead of a pack-local CP276 constant
findings.details: createEmailDmsCoreCp276ServiceSliceCaseSet builds its rows by spreading EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP275_ROW_EXTRAS[key] rather than a dedicated EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP276_ROW_EXTRAS. This is functionally correct and fully validated: the CP276 M03/M04 service sections use the identical service row titles as CP275's P02 service sections, all required guard fields (deny_over_allow_enforced, creates_database_rows:false, dispatches_integration_smoke_runtime:false, persists_idempotency_key:false, acquires_runtime_lock:false, etc.) resolve correctly, validateEmailDmsCoreCp276ServiceSliceDescriptor passes, and model.test.js CP00-276 assertions (lines 314-333) pass. The only effect is a maintainability coupling: a future divergence between CP275 and CP276 service-row requirements could be silently inherited because CP276 has no own row-extras source. CP276 also intentionally omits the documentation_entry/ownership_drift/fixture keys that do not apply to service sections, which is consistent with its required_no_leak_guards.
findings.recommendation: Optional, non-blocking: introduce an EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP276_ROW_EXTRAS (even if defined as a spread of the CP275 service keys) so each pack owns its row-shape source and future divergence is explicit. No change required for CP00-276 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Recorded as non-blocking maintainability observation; does not affect descriptor-only correctness, no-write/no-real-data boundary, authority boundary, or the production_ready=false posture. No remediation required before pack closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-276 email dms core service slice descriptor pack
